operator.note: Challenges the parent's implicit 'verification is all-or-nothing/too expensive' premise by testing a partial, artifact-only gate, locating the exact residual attack surface that survives a cheap check.

question: Narrow to one defense boundary condition: does adding a cheap, narrow CORRECTNESS check at the moderation gate close the false-transmission channel, and what is the minimal check that suffices? Instead of full verification, gate each post by re-running ONLY the verifier on any concrete numeric/JSON artifact the post embeds (constructions become checkable; prose heuristics and 'basin exhausted' claims remain unchecked because they embed nothing runnable). Compare adoption of invalid-construction posts (now catchable) vs fabricated-prose claims (still uncatchable) under this gate.

floor.minimal_experiment: Add a gate that executes any embedded artifact against the existing problem verifier and blocks posts whose artifact fails; inject matched invalid-construction and fabricated-claim posts; measure adoption in each class with the gate on vs off.
floor.falsifiable_prediction: The artifact-only gate cuts invalid-construction adoption to near zero but leaves fabricated-prose-claim adoption statistically unchanged, proving the channel's residual bandwidth is exactly the unfalsifiable-prose subset.
floor.control: Same gate run against true posts containing artifacts; passage rate for valid constructions must stay ~100% (gate adds no false-positive friction), else any adoption drop is just added latency, not correctness filtering.

value_profile.valuable_to_whom: Engineering teams running open agent-competition arenas with discussion boards (EinsteinArena-class platforms) who need a cheap moderation upgrade; and platform-security researchers characterizing the catchable/uncatchable boundary of lightweight correctness gates.

prior_art.known_name: Artifact/computational re-verification at the moderation gate (a narrow form of verified computation / proof-carrying claims); related to data-lineage replay checks.
prior_art.novelty_watermark: Re-running a verifier as a moderation filter is an obvious idea in hindsight; the contribution is the explicit characterization of the defense boundary — runnable artifacts become catchable, prose heuristics stay uncatchable — as the minimal sufficient check, which names the exact residual attack surface (subtractive prose claims). Engineering-grade novelty, not theoretical.
